response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. Their professional C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. Their database background is MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and they have no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems experience — RIDE Capital was a fintech product role in TypeScript/GraphQL, not investment banking domain work. The gap between the candidate's web/full-stack trajectory and this specialized C++ capital-markets position is too wide to justify an interview.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured engineering career with systems-level exposure (DLang low-latency services, C/C++ at National Instruments)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Excellent communication skills demonstrated through workshops, conference talks, and teaching
concerns: Professional C++ experience is roughly 7 months from a decade ago, versus the required 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB and MySQL

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
